auth_notes: DoorDash Drive API uses JWT for authentication. You must generate a token signed with an Access Key ID and a Signing Secret. The token must include specific claims (iss, aud, exp, iat) and be refreshed frequently as they typically expire within 30 minutes.
rate_limits: Approximately 300 requests per minute for most endpoints, though specific limits vary by account tier and endpoint.
gotchas: JWT generation requires a Code node in n8n to sign the payload using the crypto library, as n8n's standard Auth types don't support DoorDash's specific JWT implementation.
Address validation is extremely strict; it is recommended to use a separate address validation service before hitting the DoorDash API.
The Sandbox environment behaves differently regarding driver assignment simulation compared to Production.
research_notes: Integration requires a two-step process in n8n: 1. A Code node to generate and sign the JWT using the developer credentials. 2. An HTTP Request node using the generated token in the 'Authorization: Bearer' header. Webhooks can be configured in the DoorDash Developer Portal to send status updates to an n8n Webhook trigger node.

An HTTP Request node using the generated token in the 'Authorization: Bearer' header.
